title: Site-to-Device Ratio and Capacity Planning
dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:site:topology`. from the `site_topology` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/topology/site-topology`. Default poll interval: 3600s (1h). Volume: ~1 event per site per poll. RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `siteId` (string), `siteName` (string), `siteType` (string), `parentSiteName` (string). CIM data model: Compute_Inventory.
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:site:topology" | stats dc(deviceId) as device_count by siteId, siteName, siteType | eventstats avg(device_count) as avg_devices_per_site stdev(device_count) as stdev_devices | eval density_score=round((device_count-avg_devices_per_site)/if(stdev_devices>0,stdev_devices,1),1) | sort -device_count
description: Analyzes the distribution of devices across sites to identify over-provisioned or under-provisioned locations for capacity planning.
value: Understanding device density per site helps plan hardware refreshes, license allocation, and support staffing across locations.
implementation: Enable the `site_topology` input in the Cisco Catalyst TA pointing to `index=catalyst`. The TA polls site hierarchy data from the Catalyst Center Intent API every 60 minutes. Key fields: `siteId`, `siteType` (area, building, floor), `parentSiteName`, `siteName`, `deviceId` when present in the event payload.
visualization: Table (siteName, siteType, device_count, density_score), bar chart of device_count, scatter of density_score versus siteType.
knownFalsePositives:
**Hub sites, data centers, and stadiums naturally hosting far more devices than branch offices.** A data center aggregation site may have 100+ devices while a small branch has 3. The high ratio is expected and does not indicate a capacity problem. Distinguish by checking whether the high-device-count sites correspond to known hub or data center locations. Suppress by maintaining a `catalyst_site_capacity` lookup with expected device ranges per site type and alerting only when actual counts exceed the expected range.

**Device batch onboarding temporarily skewing the site-to-device ratio.** When many devices are onboarded simultaneously (e.g., new building commissioning), the ratio temporarily spikes for that site. Distinguish by checking whether the device count increase is recent and corresponds to a known onboarding project. Suppress by allowing a stabilization period after bulk onboarding before including the site in capacity planning alerts.

**Unassigned or staging site accumulating devices before final site assignment.** Devices discovered by Catalyst Center but not yet assigned to their final site hierarchy location accumulate in the Unassigned or staging site. Distinguish by checking whether the site name is "Unassigned" or a staging location. Do not suppress — alert on devices staying in staging for more than 7 days as an operational hygiene issue.

**Virtual devices or management interfaces counted as separate devices.** Some platforms register virtual interfaces, stack members, or management modules as separate devices in Catalyst Center, inflating the device count per site. Distinguish by checking whether multiple `deviceId` values share the same physical `hostname` prefix or `serialNumber`. Suppress by deduplicating on `hostname` or `serialNumber` for capacity planning.

references.title: Catalyst Center Network Device API — Cisco DevNet

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) with both **site** and **device** (or **devicehealth**) modular inputs enabled to `index=catalyst`.
• Catalyst Center site hierarchy and device inventory must be populated.
• RBAC: service account with **NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE**.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
This UC correlates two Catalyst Center data sources:
• **Site hierarchy:** `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:site` — polled hourly.
• **Device inventory:** `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:device` or `cisco:dnac:devicehealth` — polled every 15 minutes.

Key fields for capacity planning:
• Sites: `siteId`, `name`, `siteType`, `siteHierarchy`.
• Devices: `deviceName`, `siteId`, `deviceType`, `platformId`.
• Correlation: by `siteId` — devices are assigned to sites in the hierarchy.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:devicehealth"
| stats dc(deviceName) as device_count values(deviceType) as device_types by siteId
| lookup catalyst_site_names siteId OUTPUT site_name, siteHierarchy
| eval ratio_status=case(device_count > 200, "Overloaded", device_count > 100, "High", device_count > 50, "Medium", device_count > 0, "Normal", 1==1, "Empty")
| sort -device_count
| table site_name, siteHierarchy, device_count, device_types, ratio_status
```

Understanding this SPL:
• **`dc(deviceName) as device_count`**: Counts distinct devices per site. This is the primary capacity metric.
• **`lookup catalyst_site_names`**: Resolves `siteId` UUIDs to human-readable site names. Create this lookup from UC-5.13.51's site data: `| inputlookup catalyst_site_names.csv`.
• **`ratio_status=case(...)`**: Classifies sites by device density. Thresholds are illustrative — tune based on your site types (data centers should tolerate higher counts than branch offices).

Step 3 — Validate
• **UI comparison:** Open Catalyst Center > Assurance > Health > Device Health and select a specific site. Compare the device count with the Splunk results for the same site.
• **Hub site verification:** Confirm that known hub sites and data centers appear with high device counts as expected.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Dashboard:** Bar chart of device count by site (sorted descending), capacity classification table, and a trend chart showing device count growth per site over 30 days.
• **Alert:** Trigger when a site exceeds its expected capacity threshold (from a `catalyst_site_capacity` lookup). Route to the network planning team.
• **Growth tracking:** Compare monthly device count snapshots to identify sites approaching capacity limits.

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• **Device count includes virtual devices:** Some platforms register multiple device entries for stacks or virtual interfaces. Deduplicate by `serialNumber` or `hostname` for accurate physical device counts.
• **Site lookup missing entries:** Regenerate the `catalyst_site_names` lookup from the latest site hierarchy data.
• **No site topology events arriving:** verify the `site_topology` input is enabled and check `index=_internal` for API error or endpoint timeout messages from the TA.
• **Missing or empty values in site hierarchy:** newly created sites may have empty parent references until the hierarchy is fully configured in Catalyst Center.
• **Fewer records than expected:** check the service account role; credential access denied errors indicate insufficient permissions.
grandmaExplanation: We compare how many network devices are at each location to find places that may have too many or too few devices.
